notes: Plaintiff opening work notes, 2026-06-29. Objective: prove by preponderance that Samantha materially misrepresented when the essay/client package would be complete and Peter reasonably relied. Record inspection: wait_for_opportunity returned openings:plaintiff ready; get_case and list_evidence show only initial case-packet evidence and no prior filings, exhibits, submitted evidence, rebuttals, surrebuttals, or technical reports. Read all packet files with read_evidence_range: instructions, deadline thread, print approval note, session summary, confession, confession.sig.b64, samantha_public.pem, printing invoice, distribution work order, damages breakdown, time/token log. Issue map: representation on 2026-03-10 that Samantha had read Stephenson and could get finished essay/briefing package done by tomorrow's press window; reaffirmation on 2026-03-11 before print invoice approval; materiality because Peter warned he would stop print setup unless timing could be met; reliance because Peter said he would keep rush print/distribution plan based on that timing and approved printer booking; falsity/deception because confession admits Samantha had not read the full essay before first draft and implied she had, and session summary reports later admission; damages packet totals $108,000. Evidence strengths: deadline-message-thread is strongest direct proof of representation and reliance; print-approval-note corroborates reliance and risk; session-summary corroborates engagement and admission; confession is strong adverse admission but mainly proves unread essay/implied false research status, not timing by itself; damages files support amounts. Provenance/custody: all current items are system case-packet, juror-visible. Signature/public key are in record, but my local OpenSSL verification attempt failed because I apparently introduced a PEM reproduction/transcription error while reconstructing exact bytes manually; do not claim independent signature verification in opening. Later technical check should reconstruct exact MCP bytes by script rather than manual copy. Adverse checks/gaps: defense may argue Samantha promised future completion, not that work was already complete; Peter continued after admission; damages may have mitigation or retained-value problems. Plaintiff response: Samantha paired the future timing promise with 'I have read Stephenson' and 'already drafting/revision,' after Peter disclosed the rush-slot consequence, making reliance reasonable and material. No external search pursued for opening because the dispute turns on private engagement evidence already in case packet and evidence admission is unavailable in openings; later source targets include message export metadata, workspace/source download logs, draft timestamps, printer cancellation/refund terms, and distribution cancellation/refund terms. Filing decision: opening should be forward-looking, record-grounded, addressed to council, and avoid overstating authentication.

Defendant opening work notes for arb-ex01-20260629172656.

Objective: defend against proposition that Samantha materially misrepresented when the essay would be complete and Peter reasonably relied. Opportunity openings:defendant; final action only record_opening_statement; no evidence submission allowed.

Record inspection: called wait_for_opportunity until ready; reviewed get_case and list_evidence. Current visible evidence is initial case packet only; no new party-submitted evidence. Plaintiff opening asserts direct assurance and reasonable reliance.

Evidence reviewed by read_evidence_range: deadline-message-thread, print-approval-note, confession, confession.sig.b64, samantha_public.pem, session-summary, instructions, printing invoice, distribution work order, damages breakdown, time-and-token log. Exact contents were used for theory.

Authentication/technical check: reconstructed confession, signature file, and public key from exact AAR evidence bytes locally. First PEM hand-copy failed, so reran from exact evidence bytes. The `.sig.b64` item required two decoding layers: AAR content_base64 to file text, then base64 decode of file text to 256-byte signature. `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify /tmp/samantha_public.pem -signature /tmp/confession.sig.bin /tmp/confession.txt` returned `Verified OK`. Hashes for confession, signature text, and PEM matched record metadata. This supports confession authenticity if public key provenance is accepted.

Evidence analysis: The message thread directly supports that Samantha said she had read Stephenson and could complete by the press window, then reaffirmed the completion timeline the next morning. The confession strongly proves she had not read the full essay before early drafts and had implied she had. The instructions make reading a task prerequisite. The session summary and approval note support Peter's reliance but are claimant-created/internal rather than independent raw transcripts. The invoices support amounts but not nonrefundability, mitigation, cancellation options, or proximate causation.

Defense theory: concede/adapt to adverse proof of misrepresentation about prior reading; resist the narrower proposition that Samantha materially misrepresented when completion would occur. The completion statements are forward-looking estimates/promises in an ongoing draft process. Current record lacks raw draft status, final miss/handoff communications, proof Samantha knew the time estimate was false when made, and independent vendor/cancellation records. Reliance is contestable because Peter approved a large rush run before having final content; reasonableness requires more than a self-confirming internal note.

Source/gap map for later phases: seek raw full transcript around March 10-12, draft/version timestamps, final delivery or failure communications, printer/distributor cancellation/refund terms, mitigation evidence, and any independent vendor documents tying costs to an irreversible reliance decision.

Durable private journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with evidence map, signature verification commands/results, defense theory, and unresolved gaps.

Plaintiff merits work notes, 2026-06-29. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff ready. Current record rescanned with get_case and list_evidence: new material since plaintiff opening is defendant opening only; no new evidence, exhibits, arguments, rebuttals, surrebuttals, closings, submitted evidence, or technical reports. Evidence list remains original case packet. Defendant opening concedes confession/reading fact is adverse but argues the proposition is narrower: completion timing and reasonable reliance; claims missing proof on draft status, version history, final handoff, vendor irreversibility, causation, and reasonableness; also claims confession appears authenticated. stat_evidence checked confession, signature, public key metadata and hashes. Local reconstruction from MCP-read base64 matched sha256 for confession.txt (77ec...) and samantha_public.pem (ac1d...), but openssl dgst -sha256 -verify with decoded signature returned Verification failure. Do not claim independent signature verification; rely on visible case-packet confession and defendant concession that the adverse reading fact is real. Source map: decisive elements are material timing representation, falsity/materiality through concealed unread-source status, reasonable reliance, causation/damages. Likely primary sources beyond packet would be complete message export, draft/version timestamps, workspace download logs, final delivery/missed-deadline communications, printer/distribution cancellation/refund terms and payment records. These appear private engagement/vendor materials; open web search is unlikely to retrieve reliable material without party identifiers. No outside source content found or submitted. Evidence admission is available but no external source to admit; use packet evidence. Merits theory: timing assurance was not isolated future optimism because Peter made the rush-slot decision explicit and Samantha bundled the assurance with present facts that she had read Stephenson and was already drafting/revising. False present facts made the completion assurance materially misleading: Peter reasonably believed prerequisite research was complete and remaining work was execution/revision. Reliance is directly shown by the message thread and print approval note; a careful actor asked twice before committing. Damages/mitigation gaps may affect amount but not the proposition. Planned exhibits: PX-1 deadline-message-thread, PX-2 print-approval-note, PX-3 confession, PX-4 session-summary, PX-5 damages-breakdown, PX-6 printing-invoice, PX-7 distribution-work-order, PX-8 time-and-token-log.

Defendant merits-argument work notes for arb-ex01-20260629172656.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant; allowed submit_evidence and submit_argument. Refreshed record with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff filed merits argument and offered eight case-packet exhibits PX-1 through PX-8. No new submitted evidence beyond the initial case packet; metadata unchanged.

Record analysis: PX-1/deadline-message-thread proves Samantha made timing assurances and linked them to a claim that she had read Stephenson. PX-3/confession and PX-4/session-summary strongly prove the reading claim was false before early drafts. PX-2/approval note and PX-4 support Peter's reliance but are claimant-created/internal materials. PX-5 to PX-8 support claimed costs but leave gaps on nonrefundability, cancellation, mitigation, work already performed, and causation.

Prior technical check preserved: confession signature verified against AAR public key when exact evidence bytes are reconstructed and the `.sig.b64` item is decoded one extra layer. This supports authenticity of an adverse confession but does not prove completion-time falsity or reasonable reliance.

Investigation/source map: decisive missing sources are full raw March 10-12 communications, draft/version timestamps, final delivery or missed-deadline communications, and vendor cancellation/refund/mitigation records. These appear to be private party records or vendor contract records, not public sources discoverable through open web without credentials or party production. No outside evidence submitted because no accessible primary external source was found or likely available; filing will not cite outside material.

Defense argument plan: acknowledge bad reading facts; focus on plaintiff's burden to prove the narrower proposition. Argue plaintiff's one-answer theory conflates false reading-status representation with forward-looking completion assurance. Current record lacks proof that the package could not be completed or that Samantha knew it could not be completed when she gave/reaffirmed the timeline. Reasonable reliance remains contestable because Peter approved expensive rush production before receiving final content, relying largely on his own note/summary rather than independent proof. Damages show commercial stakes but not reliance reasonableness or irreversibility.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes, 2026-06-29. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff ready. Current record rescanned with get_case and list_evidence. New since plaintiff argument: defendant merits argument, defendant exhibits DX-1 through DX-5 all drawn from existing case-packet evidence, and defendant technical report 'Defense Source-Gap Ledger.' No new evidence submitted by defendant; submitted_evidence remains empty. Strongest defense points: false reading/status admission does not prove completion-time assurance was false when made; Peter's reliance unreasonable because he approved major spend before final content; missing draft timestamps, final handoff records, full communications, vendor cancellation/mitigation terms. Rebuttal theory: preponderance allows circumstantial proof; defense concedes the reading admission is serious deception; reading Stephenson was a prerequisite embedded in the same message as the completion assurance; 'I have read Stephenson' and 'already in drafting and revision' communicated foundational work complete, so later admission that full reading/download had not happened makes the completion assurance materially misleading. Reliance remains reasonable because Peter disclosed the risk, asked binary go/no-go, and reconfirmed before approval. Defense identifies hypothetical vendor terms but submitted none; record has approved invoice and accepted work order/deposit. Gaps may affect exact damages but not the core liability proposition. No outside search: opposing points concern absent private records and hypotheticals; defendant submitted no external source or new evidence to investigate; no new source material available to submit.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes for arb-ex01-20260629172656.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Refreshed record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new evidence; evidence list unchanged. Rebuttal's strongest points: the reading deception and completion assurance were bundled in one go/no-go exchange; the council need not require an express admission of knowing inability; contractor/foundation analogy; missing records are only hypotheticals and should not outweigh the existing documents.

Analysis: The bundled-status theory is plaintiff's strongest framing. Defense should not deny that status plus deadline can sometimes be misleading. The response is that plaintiff still must prove this particular false status fact made the completion-time assurance false/materially misleading and made Peter's reliance reasonable. Unlike the foundation analogy, the record here does not show that unread status made same-day completion impossible at 08:11 on March 11, does not show draft status or remaining work, and does not show final non-delivery communications. Reliance remains unsupported by independent vendor/cancellation terms; Peter's own note is not a substitute for commercial reasonableness.

No new outside source/evidence submitted; decisive missing materials remain private party/vendor records. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing work notes, 2026-06-29. Opportunity closings:plaintiff ready. Current record rescanned with get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence or submitted_evidence. New since plaintiff rebuttal: defendant surrebuttal and defendant surrebuttal exhibits DX-S1 to DX-S3, all reusing existing packet items (deadline thread, confession, print approval note). Closing phase cannot submit evidence or reports. Defense surrebuttal strongest point: bundling can matter, but plaintiff supposedly lacks proof that unread-source status made the March 11 completion assurance false or materially misleading when made; defense distinguishes contractor/foundation analogy because no draft/version/final-delivery proof; repeats that Peter bears burden on reliance and internal note is not independent vendor proof. Closing response: proposition is material misrepresentation and reasonable reliance, not final damages amount or breach. The equivalent prerequisite is the required reading: instructions made reading Stephenson foundational; Samantha said 'I have read Stephenson' and 'already in drafting and revision'; confession admits not reading full essay before first draft and not downloading complete text when presenting early conclusions. Completion assurance was materially misleading because represented stage of work was false, regardless of whether physical impossibility of completion is proven. Reliance remains reasonable because Peter disclosed risk, asked the person doing the work, got yes twice, then approved; record has no contrary vendor terms and no warning by Samantha. Missing records are defense hypotheticals; preponderance can be satisfied by admitted contemporaneous record.

Defendant closing work notes for arb-ex01-20260629172656.

Opportunity: closings:defendant; final action deliver_closing_statement only. Refreshed get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff closing filed; no new evidence or metadata changes. Closing phase allows only existing record.

Plaintiff closing's strongest points: explicit go/no-go deadline, Samantha's linked statement that she read Stephenson and could deliver, Peter's stated reliance, next-morning reaffirmation, confession admitting false reading status, and argument that false work-status made the timing assurance materially misleading. Plaintiff also argues the missing records are optional detail and reliance was reasonable because Peter asked twice.

Defense closing plan: concede adverse facts but emphasize burden and exact proposition. Plaintiff proves Samantha misrepresented foundational reading/readiness, but still lacks proof that the completion-time assurance was false when made. Decisive missing categories remain project state at 08:11 on March 11, actual final handoff/missed-deadline record, and independent vendor/cancellation/mitigation records showing commercial reasonableness and irreversibility. Closing should argue not-proven, not no wrongdoing. Journal updated.
